objective: Implement the next narrow child under issue #615, bounded to local governance-hook consumption of the merged #625 degraded-fallback proof contract.
scope_boundary: Owns the bounded local governance-hook degraded-fallback proof consumer slice.
Keeps fallback-log schema/workflow parity under #612, planning-authority work under #607, and downstream verifier work under #614 out of scope.
Keeps the dirty primary checkout isolated by working only inside the issue-627 worktree.
out_of_scope: Fallback-log schema or workflow parity surfaces under issue #612.
Execution-scope schema/assert/test surfaces already merged through issue #625.
Issue #614 downstream verifier or drift-gate work.
Broad hook rewrites beyond the smallest root-hook/helper path needed for this child.
research_summary: Post-merge review of issues #612, #615, #607, #614, and #625 showed the next narrow residual was the local governance-hook consumer path that must honor the merged #625 degraded-fallback proof contract. This child therefore stays under #615 and owns only hooks/governance-check.sh, the smallest shared helper path needed to consume that proof locally, focused tests, and required issue-local governance artifacts.

sprints.name: Intake And Ownership Boundary
sprints.goal: Keep issue #627 narrower than the remaining parent surfaces while promoting it into a bounded implementation slice.
tasks: Review docs/PROGRESS.md, issue #615, issue #625, and the current governance-hook surfaces.
Record the exact owned governance-hook/helper surfaces and the explicit out-of-scope #612, #607, and #614 surfaces.
deliverables: State the exact owned governance-hook/helper surfaces.
State the explicit out-of-scope fallback-log parity, planning-authority, and downstream verifier surfaces.
acceptance_criteria: The implementation artifacts state that issue #627 is the next narrow child under issue #615 after merged issue #625.
The implementation artifacts name only hooks/governance-check.sh, the smallest required helper path, focused tests, and required issue-local governance artifacts as the owned surface.
The implementation artifacts explicitly exclude fallback-log schema/workflow parity and broader validator/workflow work under issue #612.

sprints.name: Local Hook Proof Contract
sprints.goal: Return the exact local fail-closed proof for the bounded implementation slice.
tasks: Require local root-hook fail-closed behavior when the merged #625 proof is missing, blank, unsafe, or malformed.
Require at least one allowed path proof showing compliant local governance changes still pass.
Keep the proof contract local-hook specific and avoid redefining issue #625 execution-scope semantics.
deliverables: Define the local negative fail-closed classes.
Define the local positive allowed-path proof requirement.
Require parity with the merged #625 proof contract rather than a new contract.
acceptance_criteria: The implementation artifacts name exact negative local governance-hook failure classes tied to the merged #625 proof contract.
The implementation artifacts require at least one positive allowed-path proof.
The implementation artifacts do not widen into fallback-log schema/workflow or broader packet-routing enforcement.

specialist_reviews.focus: Verify the next valid child after issue #625 merge and keep parent boundaries explicit.
specialist_reviews.summary: The next child should remain under issue #615 and own only the local root-hook consumer path for the merged issue #625 degraded-fallback proof contract.

specialist_reviews.focus: Verify the minimum planning packet, handoff, and execution-scope fields required before issue #627 implementation can start.
specialist_reviews.summary: The planning packet needs the canonical structured plan, planning execution scope, accepted handoff, issue/branch alignment, and standard packet validators before implementation can start.

alternate_model_review.summary: Sanctioned alternate-family implementation review approved the bounded issue-627 code slice. Non-blocking notes were limited to adding a focused nonexistent-file regression and keeping the helper as a pure discovery-and-replay shim.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #627 may implement only the local governance-check consumer path for the merged #625 degraded-fallback proof contract under issue #615.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Finalize issue-local validation, closeout, local-ci proof, and publish the bounded implementation slice without widening into #612/#607/#614.
handoff_acceptance_criteria: Issue #627 remains bounded to the local root-hook degraded-fallback proof consumer path only.
Issue #627 consumes the merged #625 proof contract rather than redefining it.
Issue #627 does not claim #612, #615, #607, or #614 closure by itself.

material_deviation_rules: Any patch outside hooks/governance-check.sh, scripts/overlord/check_governance_hook_execution_scope.py, focused tests, required governance mirror/doc co-staging, hook-generated graphify writeback, or issue-local artifacts is a material deviation from issue #627 scope.
Any edit to fallback-log schema/workflow parity surfaces is a material deviation from issue #627 scope.
Any claim that issue #627 closes issue #615 or issue #612 by itself is a material deviation.
